purpose: Same-commit A/B promotion regression: rerun qwen-plus baseline and dated Qwen 3.7 candidate on one already-observed developer-labelled synthetic set. This is not held-out, confirmatory, independent, or statistically powered evidence.
datasetStatus: Previously observed, synthetic, developer-labelled 48-pair regression set. Historical qwen-plus v1.1 outcomes were known before this protocol.
executionOrderInterpretation: Deterministic counterbalance by repetition: AB / BA / AB. Each arm runs once per repetition over the same frozen embeddings.

arms.judgeMaxTokens: omitted: Model Studio structured JSON mode must not set max_tokens
omitted: Model Studio structured JSON mode must not set max_tokens

sdk.retryObservability: The OpenAI-compatible SDK may retry internally. This runner records the configured ceiling and application-level calls, but does not claim to observe SDK-internal retry attempts.

failureScoring: Inconclusive is incorrect for accuracy; inconclusive positives count as false negatives; completion is reported separately. Every failed case remains in its arm and repetition.
reporting: Every embedding result, arm, case, repetition, miss, error, aggregate and gate is appended to one attempt event stream; no attempt, arm, repetition, or case is selected or discarded.

attemptPolicy.allowedDirtyEvidencePattern: Only prior bench/results/*promotion*.json or *.jsonl evidence; every allowed path is recorded. No source, protocol, config, package, or other path may be dirty.
attemptPolicy.sameCommitGuarantee: Both arms execute inside one attempt after one repository snapshot.

promotionRule: Promote only when both arms complete all three fixed-order repetitions, both pass the versioned absolute gate, and the candidate passes every versioned non-regression delta against the same-attempt baseline. The dataset was already observed before this protocol.
comparisonPolicy: Report per-run matrices, arm mean/range, raw paired run deltas, and the mechanical gate. Do not call repetitions independent samples or claim statistical significance, held-out generalization, production prevalence, or superiority beyond this observed set.
